question: The protagonist of the story "Storm in a Teacup" obsessively reads a book with this number in its title. This number partly titles a work that opens by noting that people are born naturally good, used to teach children. One hundred times this number titles a poetry anthology whose length was inspired by the Classic of Poetry. A man visits a hut this number of times to recruit the Sleeping Dragon, and this many characters pledge to die on the same day while swearing the Peach Garden Oath. For 10 points, a Luo Guanzhong novel is titled for how many kingdoms?

question: This painting inspired a recently resurfaced Magritte painting of his wife outdoors with a conch shell on her stomach. An essay on the oppositional gaze of a figure who seems to disappear into this painting's background is partly titled for Black female subjectivity. This painting inspired one in which a man sitting on a couch by a red table looks up at a curled-up woman being draped with cloth. Paul Cézanne made a "modern" version of this painting, whose central nude wears a flower and a black ribbon, and receives a bouquet of flowers from a Black maid. For 10 points, name this Edouard Manet painting of a woman reclining in bed.

question: A character with this first name is scammed out of his money after becoming a clergyman and marries a maid who turns out to be an alcoholic bigamist in a novel. A character with this name is rebutted by Gilbert in an essay on the importance of criticism. A woman accidentally gets a copy of The Green Carnation while searching lists for a general with this first name. This name "inspires absolute confidence" in the fiancée of a character found by Miss Prism in Victoria Station, who tells Algernon that he uses this name in the city and Jack Worthing in the country. For 10 points, an Oscar Wilde play is titled for the "Importance" of a trait that puns on what name?
